public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as void pilgrim.
appearance: Arin Field usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their void pilgrim status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Carries the habits of a place-bound worker whose judgment comes from repetition rather than theory. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a void pilgrim still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of void pilgrim in and around Shade Moon, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Shade Moon workable through the obligations of void pilgrim while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must keep their piece of the world working while larger systems push stress downward. Around Shade Moon, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of void pilgrim visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Arin Field can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Arin Field can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: Arin Field carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shade Moon: the part of Shade Moon where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Arin Field presents as Carries the habits of a place-bound worker whose judgment comes from repetition rather than theory. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a void pilgrim still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Void Pilgrim, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Arin Field refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Void Pilgrim too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Arin Field can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Arin Field adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Arin Field is Known locally through day-to-day work as void pilgrim. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Void Pilgrim.
